On July 7, 1937, the massive, incredibly horrific, and deeply consequential Marco Polo Bridge Incident aggressively occurred near Beijing, China. This massive, unprovoked escalation directly and absolutely triggered the incredibly brutal, massive Second Sino-Japanese War, which seamlessly merged into the absolute devastation of the Pacific Theatre of World War II.

This massive, incredibly controversial annexation completely destroyed the absolute sovereignty of the independent Kingdom of Hawaii, heavily securing an absolute, dominant American naval and economic foothold in the central Pacific Ocean.

## SECTION 9 — This Day in History (India)
On July 7, 1999, during the incredibly massive, brutal, and highly strategic Kargil War, the Indian Armed Forces achieved one of their most absolute, heroic, and incredibly immortalized victories. Captain Vikram Batra, leading a massive, fiercely determined assault against heavily fortified Pakistani bunkers on Point 4875, was brutally and aggressively killed in action while selflessly saving a fellow officer. His absolute, uncompromising bravery and his iconic, massive radio transmission "Yeh Dil Maange More!" heavily cemented his legacy as a massive, legendary martyr of the Indian Republic.

On July 7, 1948, the massive, heavily strategic, and absolute foundational infrastructure of the modern Indian state was aggressively expanded. The Damodar Valley Corporation (DVC) was officially and formally established by an Act of the Constituent Assembly. Heavily modeled upon the massive Tennessee Valley Authority in the US, the DVC was the absolute, very first multipurpose river valley project of independent India, aggressively designed to heavily generate massive hydroelectric power, provide crucial irrigation, and fiercely control the devastating, massive floods in the Bengal and Bihar regions.
